title: dns issue: aardvark-dns network namespace is misconfigured when running as root
body:
### Issue Description

This is - I believe - basically the same issue as #20396 (that was closed) - I  believe - though in my use case the issue is definitely only happening when running as root.

I'm setting up systemd containers in `/etc/containers/systemd` with a `.network` configuration, and when using the configured network - containers cannot resolve DNS addresses at all: messages to port 53 time out.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue

Here's the repro of the test run asked for by @Luap99 in issue #20396:

```
root@normandi:~# podman network create testnet
testnet
root@normandi:~# podman run --network testnet --name c1 -d  alpine sleep inf
a9072f566bdb5bb62b0e76d4bbc2c9636ecca639b71508d706fd1ab07f30b992
root@normandi:~# podman run --network testnet --rm alpine nslookup c1
;; connection timed out; no servers could be reached

root@normandi:~# podman run --network testnet --rm alpine nslookup google.com
;; connection timed out; no servers could be reached

```


### Describe the results you received

DNS timeout failure

### Describe the results you expected

DNS should resolve properly

### podman info output

```yaml

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

The default installation for my system - Ubuntu LTS 24.04 - is version 4.9.3, which exhibits this problem.

Running the `readlink /proc/…` steps (as in issue #20396), it is showing that `aardvark-dns` is running in the host's namespace (same net id for `aardvark-dns` as for `self`).

A local installation the latest 5.8.0 release does not exhibit this problem - with the test scenario detailed above, but this does not help with running the systemd containers. Reviewing the namespace configuration as per the `readlink /proc/…` commands - shows the same behavior with 5.8.0: the `aardvark-dns` process is on the same namespace as the host.

### Additional information

I understand if the answer is going to be - WONTFIX, please update your ancient podman version.

comments.body:
I have updated my system to podman 5.7.0 and still encounter the same issue. 

Here's an updated `podman info`:

```

> the aardvark-dns process is on the same namespace as the host.

Because that is the expected behavior. When running as root aardvark-dns lsitens onm the host on the bridge port 53 by default. If it cannot receive packages there check your firewall settings my guess would be you block these connections.

So you are saying that in rootless mode there's no need to configure the firewall to allow access from the container to the podman DNS service, but in privileged mode the firewall should be taken into account?

Mangling the firewall seems to solve the problem. I added:

```
ufw allow proto udp from 10.20.30.0/24 to 10.20.30.1 port 53
ufw allow proto udp from fd10::20:30:0/112 to fd10::20:30:1 port 53
ufw route allow from 10.20.30.0/24 to any
```

And that allowed DNS queries to complete. Without the `route allow` rule, the containers aren't able to access anything outside their own network, even if they can resolve it.

Because as rootless we run inside the "rootless netns" which has no admin firewall rules where it would conflict with
https://github.com/containers/podman/discussions/22943#discussioncomment-9795883

As root because the interfaces are created on the host aardvark-dns must run there as well and then the host firewall can block it depending on the config.
